grammar: "Minnas" is deponent — active meaning (remember) but -s form.
"Trivas" = to thrive/enjoy being somewhere. No non-s form exists.

english: They breathe heavily after the run.
grammar: "Andas" is deponent — always has -s but is active in meaning (to breathe).

english: I'm not visible in the dark.
grammar: "Synas" = to be visible/appear. Deponent.

english: We always succeed in the end.
grammar: "Lyckas" (succeed) is deponent. "I slutändan" = in the end.
"Misslyckas" (fail) — prefix miss- + lyckas. Many Swedish verbs build meaning with prefixes.
"Umgås" = spend time together. Very important concept in Swedish social life.
"Följas åt" = go together / accompany each other.
"Mötas" = meet each other (reciprocal). "Möta" = meet someone.
Here "-s" makes it passive: 'the door is opened.' Context tells passive from deponent.

english: The children are played with by other children.

grammar: "Enas om" = agree on. Past: "enades." Reciprocal s-verb.

english: They fight for the last spot.
grammar: "Slåss" = to fight. Deponent verb with active meaning.
"Kramas" = to hug each other. The reciprocal s-form.
"Brottas" = wrestle. Reciprocal — they wrestle each other.
"Träffas" = meet each other (by arrangement).

english: They help each other with the move.

target: De turas om att laga mat.
grammar: "Turas om" = take turns. Deponent.

english: We part ways at the intersection.
She is ashamed of the mistake.
grammar: "Skämmas" = to be ashamed. Deponent — no non-s form.
"Fattas" = be missing/lack. Also impersonal: "Det fattas en sak" (one thing is missing).
"Finnas" = to exist / be located. "Det finns" = there is/are.

target: Det finns många sjöar i Sverige.
english: There are many lakes in Sweden.

target: Det verkas som att det stämmer.

english: We are amused by the entertainment.

grammar: "Födas" = to be born. Technically passive but used as deponent.

english: The child is baptized in the church.
They get engaged on New Year's Eve.
She is hit by a cold.
They are fascinated by the northern lights.
I am touched by the music.
We are bothered by the heat.
He is confused by the instructions.
We are motivated by the results.
They are impressed by the performance.
We are surprised by the answer.
I am worried by the news.
He is not ashamed at all.
We hope to be able to help.

target: Jag minns att vi träffades där.
english: I remember that we met there.
They enjoyed themselves at the cabin.

target: De träffades första gången i Lund.
english: They met for the first time in Lund.
We hugged for a long time.
I hope we see each other soon.

target: De kände att de fattades något.
english: They felt that something was missing.
We really enjoyed ourselves on vacation.
I can't manage to open the door.

target: Hon minns inte var hon lade nycklarna.
english: She doesn't remember where she put the keys.

target: Vi möttes av en vacker soluppgång.
english: We were met by a beautiful sunrise.

target: De kramas alltid när de ses.
english: They always hug when they see each other.
He tries not to be ashamed.
We walked together in the park.
It turns out to be true.

target: Vi hoppas alla på det bästa.
english: We all hope for the best.

target: Jag ska åka till Norge imorgon.
english: I'm going to go to Norway tomorrow.
grammar: "Ska" + infinitive expresses plans/intentions. The most common future form.

english: It is going to rain tonight.
grammar: "Kommer att" + infinitive expresses predictions, especially weather and external events.

target: Vi skulle flytta om vi kunde.
english: We would move if we could.
grammar: "Skulle" + infinitive = would. Used for hypotheticals and conditionals.

target: Hon ska börja ett nytt jobb.
english: She is going to start a new job.
grammar: "Ska" + infinitive is the most common future in Swedish. More definite than "kommer att."

target: Det kommer att bli kallt i morgon.
grammar: "Kommer att" + infinitive = will. More neutral/predictive than "ska" (which implies intention).

target: Jag skulle vilja ha en kaffe.
grammar: "Skulle vilja" = would like. Very polite way to make requests.

target: Vi ska gifta oss i sommar.
english: We are getting married this summer.
grammar: "Ska" for planned future events. "I sommar" means this/next summer.

target: Det kommer att ta tre timmar.
grammar: "Ta" in time expressions: "Det tar 5 min" (it takes 5 min). Idiomatic and very common.
"Skulle" = would. Also used for reported future: "Han sa att han skulle komma."
"Ska" for definite plans/intentions. More personal than "kommer att" which is prediction.
"Kommer att" for predictions/expectations. "Ska" would imply you've already arranged help.

target: Om jag vann på lotto skulle jag resa.
english: If I won the lottery I would travel.
grammar: Swedish conditional: "om" + past tense, "skulle" + infinitive. Same pattern as English.

english: They are going to move to Malmö.
grammar: "Ska" for future plans someone has decided on. "Malmö" is Sweden's third-largest city.
"Kommer att bli" = will become/get. "Bli" is the go-to verb for changes of state.
"Skulle kunna" = could (conditional). More polite than "kan."

target: Vi ska ha fest i helgen.
english: We're having a party this weekend.
grammar: "I helgen" = this weekend. "Ha fest" = have a party — no article before "fest."
"Kommer att vara" = will be (state). Use "bli" for change, "vara" for ongoing state.

english: She would like to travel more.
grammar: "Skulle vilja" = would like to. The polite way to express wishes.

target: Jag ska prova den nya restaurangen.
english: I'm going to try the new restaurant.
grammar: "Prova" = try. "Ska prova" = am going to try (planned action).
"Kommer att bli" = will become/be. Prediction about the future.

target: Vi skulle stanna längre om vi kunde.
english: We would stay longer if we could.
grammar: "Om vi kunde" = if we could. Hypothetical condition: past tense in Swedish.

english: He's going to study to be a doctor.
grammar: "Plugga" = study (informal). "Plugga till" = study to become.

target: Vi ska resa till Japan nästa år.
english: We're going to travel to Japan next year.

target: Om jag hade tid skulle jag läsa mer.
english: If I had time I would read more.
I'm going to start exercising regularly.

target: Jag skulle vilja lära mig fler språk.
english: I would like to learn more languages.

target: Han skulle kunna komma på fredag.
Jag ska handla på vägen hem.
english: I'm going to shop on the way home.

target: Tågen kommer att gå som vanligt.
english: The trains will run as usual.

target: Vi skulle bli glada om ni kom.
english: We would be happy if you came.

target: Hon ska ta semester i augusti.
english: She's going to take vacation in August.

target: Jag skulle inte ha gjort det.
grammar: "Skulle inte ha" + supine = wouldn't have. Conditional perfect.

target: Vi ska fira med hela familjen.
english: We're going to celebrate with the whole family.

target: Han skulle ha kommit om han kunnat.
english: He would have come if he could.
I'm going to book tickets today.

target: Det kommer snart att vara vår.
Vi skulle vilja bo vid havet.
english: We would like to live by the sea.

target: Hon ska börja plugga i höst.
english: She's going to start studying in the fall.

target: Om jag var du skulle jag vänta.
english: If I were you I would wait.

grammar: "Skulle ha" + supine also = should have (in hindsight).

english: He's going to cook dinner tonight.

target: Vi ska besöka mormor på söndag.
english: We're going to visit grandma on Sunday.

target: Det kommer att bli en lång vinter.
english: It will be a long winter.

target: Om vi hade pengar skulle vi resa.
english: If we had money we would travel.
I'm going to buy a new bicycle.

target: Han skulle inte ha sagt det.
Vi ska åka och fiska i helgen.
Jag skulle vilja prova att segla.
english: I would like to try sailing.
She's going to get a driver's license.

target: Vi skulle kunna ta tåget istället.
english: We could take the train instead.

target: Jag ska anmäla mig till kursen.
english: I'm going to sign up for the course.

target: Det kommer att bli möjligt snart.
Om det inte regnar ska vi grilla.
english: If it doesn't rain we'll barbecue.
We're going to renovate the bathroom.
They're going to get a dog.

target: Det kommer att ta lite tid.
Jag ska lära mig att laga husmanskost.
english: I'm going to learn to cook Swedish home cooking.
grammar: "Husmanskost" is traditional Swedish home cooking — hearty, comforting dishes.

target: Det kommer att vara värt besväret.
english: It will be worth the trouble.
We're going to plant new trees.

target: Jag skulle kunna tänka mig det.
grammar: "Jag skulle kunna tänka mig" = I could imagine / I'd be open to — very common polite phrase.

target: Vi ska ta itu med problemet.
english: We're going to tackle the problem.
He would like to live abroad.

target: Jag ska läsa klart boken i helgen.
english: I'm going to finish the book this weekend.

target: Sverige kommer att nå sina klimatmål.
english: Sweden will reach its climate goals.

target: Om jag kunde skulle jag stanna.
english: If I could I would stay.
We're going to have a nice time.

target: Det kommer att finnas fler möjligheter.
Jag skulle vilja tacka er alla.
english: I would like to thank you all.

target: Vi ska göra det här tillsammans.
english: We're going to do this together.

target: Allt ska bli bra till slut.
english: Everything will be fine in the end.

target: Vi skulle inte ha missat det.
Jag ska börja om på nytt.
Det kommer aldrig att hända igen.
Vi ska ta vara på varje dag.
english: We're going to treasure every day.

target: Hon skulle ha föredragit att stanna.
english: She would have preferred to stay.

target: Jag skulle vilja se mer av Sverige.
english: I would like to see more of Sweden.

target: Även om det regnar ska vi gå ut.
english: Even if it rains we'll go out.
grammar: "Även om" = even if/though. Introduces a concessive clause.

target: Å ena sidan är det billigt, å andra sidan är det långt bort.
english: On one hand it's cheap, on the other hand it's far away.
grammar: "Å ena sidan ... å andra sidan" = on one hand ... on the other hand.
"Dessutom" = moreover/besides/in addition.

target: Trots att hon var trött fortsatte hon.
grammar: "Trots att" = despite the fact that. Subclause triggers verb-final order.

target: Antingen åker vi idag eller imorgon.
english: Either we go today or tomorrow.
grammar: "Antingen ... eller" = either ... or.
"Varken ... eller" = neither ... nor.

target: Dels regnade det, dels blåste det.
english: Partly it rained, partly it was windy.
grammar: "Dels ... dels" = partly ... partly. Used to list reasons.

target: Ju mer man övar, desto bättre blir man.
english: The more you practice, the better you get.
grammar: "Ju...desto" = the more...the more. A fixed correlative pattern.

target: Inte bara studerar hon, hon jobbar också.
english: Not only does she study, she also works.
grammar: "Inte bara ... utan också" = not only ... but also.

target: Huruvida det stämmer vet jag inte.
english: Whether it's correct I don't know.
grammar: "Huruvida" = whether. More formal than "om."

target: Förutsatt att vädret tillåter åker vi.
english: Provided the weather permits, we'll go.
grammar: "Förutsatt att" = provided that / assuming that.

target: I och med att han slutade fick hon jobbet.
english: Since he quit, she got the job.
grammar: "I och med att" = since/given that.

target: Så länge du är här är jag glad.
english: As long as you're here I'm happy.
grammar: "Så länge" = as long as. Second clause inverts after fronted subclause.

target: Snarare tycker jag det är intressant.
grammar: "Snarare" = rather/more accurately.
"Emellertid" = however. More formal than "men."

english: On the other hand, I like coffee.
grammar: "Däremot" = on the other hand / by contrast.
"Likväl" = nevertheless/yet. Formal.

target: Visserligen är det dyrt, men det är bra.
english: Admittedly it's expensive, but it's good.
grammar: "Visserligen...men" = admittedly...but. Concessive pattern.

target: I synnerhet gillar jag svensk natur.
english: In particular I like Swedish nature.
grammar: "I synnerhet" = especially/in particular. Formal connector.

target: Till följd av stormen stängdes vägen.
english: As a result of the storm, the road was closed.

target: Med anledning av festen stänger vi tidigt.
english: On account of the party, we close early.

target: Bortsett från vädret var det perfekt.
english: Apart from the weather, it was perfect.

grammar: "Såvida ... inte" = unless.

target: I den mån det är möjligt hjälper vi.
english: Insofar as it's possible, we'll help.

target: Allt eftersom tiden gick blev det bättre.
english: As time went on it got better.
grammar: "Allt eftersom" = as (gradually over time).

target: Oavsett vad som händer fortsätter vi.
english: Regardless of what happens, we continue.

target: I gengäld hjälper jag dig imorgon.
english: In return, I'll help you tomorrow.

grammar: "Dock" = however/yet. Very common in written Swedish.

english: Namely, the problem is more complex.

target: Med hänsyn till situationen väntar vi.
english: In consideration of the situation, we'll wait.

target: Å andra sidan har du en poäng.
english: On the other hand, you have a point.

target: I samband med flytten behöver vi hjälp.
english: In connection with the move, we need help.

target: I stället för att klaga, föreslå en lösning.
english: Instead of complaining, suggest a solution.

target: Utan tvekan är det rätt beslut.
english: Without doubt, it's the right decision.

grammar: "Följaktligen" = consequently/therefore. Formal.

target: Sammanfattningsvis var det en bra dag.
english: In summary, it was a good day.

target: Annorlunda uttryckt, vi har ett problem.
english: Put differently, we have a problem.

target: Med andra ord behöver vi agera nu.
english: In other words, we need to act now.

target: Det vill säga, vi har två alternativ.
english: That is to say, we have two options.
grammar: "Det vill säga" (dvs.) = that is (i.e.).

target: Nämligen, det finns en annan lösning.
Allteftersom vintern närmade sig blev det kallare.
english: As winter approached it got colder.

target: Inte desto mindre är jag optimistisk.
grammar: "Inte desto mindre" = nonetheless/nevertheless.

target: Så till vida att vi har pengar kvar.
english: In so far as we have money left.

target: I avsaknad av bevis friade rätten.
english: In the absence of evidence, the court acquitted.

target: Till skillnad från Stockholm är Malmö platt.
I jämförelse med förra året har vi förbättrats.
english: Compared to last year, we have improved.

target: Med avseende på kvaliteten är den utmärkt.
english: With regard to quality, it's excellent.
Notwithstanding the problems, the project was completed.
grammar: "Oaktat" = notwithstanding. Very formal written Swedish.

target: Emedan solen sken var alla glada.
english: As the sun shone, everyone was happy.
grammar: "Emedan" = as/since. Archaic/formal — rarely used in speech.

target: Så fort dörren öppnades rusade hunden ut.
english: As soon as the door opened, the dog rushed out.

target: Inte förrän klockan tre fick vi svar.
english: Not until three o'clock did we get an answer.
Given the circumstances, it was good.

target: I enlighet med reglerna agerade vi.
english: In accordance with the rules, we acted.

target: Med utgångspunkt från data kan vi se.
english: Based on data, we can see.

target: Sett till resultaten har vi lyckats.
english: Looking at the results, we have succeeded.

target: Så vitt jag vet stämmer det.
english: As far as I know, it's correct.

target: Såsom jag ser det finns det inget val.
english: As I see it, there is no choice.

target: I det fall att ni behöver hjälp, ring.
english: In case you need help, call.

target: För det första behöver vi planera.
english: First of all, we need to plan.

target: För det andra ska vi budgetera.

english: Finally, I want to thank everyone.
In addition to that, there are further costs.

target: Likaså gäller det för alla avdelningar.
english: Likewise, it applies to all departments.

target: På samma sätt kan vi lösa det här.
english: In the same way, we can solve this.
At the same time, we must be realistic.

target: Tvärtemot vad vi trodde gick det bra.
english: Contrary to what we thought, it went well.

target: Tvärtom tycker jag det är en bra idé.
english: On the contrary, I think it's a good idea.

target: Istället för att vänta agerade vi.
Det innebär att vi behöver ändra planen.
english: This means we need to change the plan.
Hereby I declare the meeting open.

target: Härav följer att vi måste agera.
english: From this it follows that we must act.

target: I det stora hela var det positivt.
english: On the whole, it was positive.

target: Så att säga var det en kompromiss.
english: So to speak, it was a compromise.

target: I alla händelser kommer vi att vara redo.
english: In any case, we will be ready.
The fact is that we succeeded.

target: Allt som allt var det en bra dag.
english: All in all, it was a good day.
Just the same, we moved forward.
Even more remarkable is the success.

target: I korthet handlar det om samarbete.

english: Anyhow, we need to move on.

target: Med det sagt vill jag tacka er.
english: With that said, I want to thank you.

target: Utöver det har vi inga fler frågor.
english: Beyond that, we have no more questions.

target: I ljuset av nya fakta ändrar vi oss.
english: In light of new facts, we change our minds.

target: Mot bakgrund av detta föreslår jag.
I första hand bör vi fokusera.
english: In the first place, we should focus.

target: I andra hand kan vi överväga.

english: Above all, I want to emphasize.
In addition to this, travel costs are added.

target: I övrigt finns inget att tillägga.
english: Otherwise, there is nothing to add.

target: Det ska tilläggas att vi är nöjda.
english: It should be added that we are satisfied.
In practical terms, this means that.

target: Sett ur det perspektivet stämmer det.
english: Seen from that perspective, it's correct.
As a last resort, the boss decides.

grammar: Present participle as adjective: -ande/-ende. "Le" → "leende." Does not change for gender.
Present participle as adjective: sjungande (singing). Same form for en/ett/plural.
Participles don't inflect: springande works with any gender/number.
Present participle (-ande/-ende) never changes form — same for all genders.
Present participle is invariable — same form for en/ett/plural: "en/ett/de växande..."
Past participle as adjective: changes for gender! En-word: -d. Ett-word: -t. Plural: -da.
Past participle as adjective: stängd/stängt/stängda. Agrees with the noun's gender/number.
Plural past participle: -a ending. Stängd (en) → stängt (ett) → stängda (plural/definite).
Past participle as adj agrees with gender: parkerad (en), parkerat (ett), parkerade (pl/def).
Strong verbs have irregular past participles: "bryta" → "bruten/brutet/brutna."
"Skriven/skrivet/skrivna" — strong verb participle. "Ord" is ett-word, plural same form.
Strong verb past participle: skriven (en), skrivet (ett), skrivna (plural).
Past participle as adjective: "målad" (painted). "Målat" for ett-words: "det målade huset."
"Kokt" = boiled/cooked. Past participle agrees: kokt (ett), kokta (plural/definite).
"Fryst" = frozen (from "frysa"). Indefinite ett-word: "ett fryst paket" (no change needed).
"Älskade" can mean both "beloved" (participle adjective) and "loved" (past tense). Context decides.
Past participle with en-word (indefinite): "respekterad." No ending change needed.
"Förlorad" = lost (from "förlora"). "Pass" is ett-word: passet. "Förlorade" in definite.
"Glömd" = forgotten. Plural definite: "glömda." "Minne" is ett-word, plural: "minnen."
"Överraskande" (surprising) — present participle (-ande/-ende) never inflects. Same for all forms.
"Avslappnande" = relaxing. Present participle from "avslappna." -ande is universal.
"Fascinerande" = fascinating. Present participle — never changes form.
"Utmanande" = challenging. From "utmana" (challenge).
"Inspirerande" = inspiring. "Tal" is ett-word: ett tal, talet, tal, talen.

english: The girl sitting by the window.
The man standing in the corner.
The children playing in the garden.

grammar: "Levande" = living (present participle). "Leva" → "levande" — a very common form.
"Anställd" (employed) functions as both participle and noun.
"Angående" = regarding. A present participle used as a preposition.

target: Han sa att han var trött.
english: He said that he was tired.
grammar: Reported speech: "sa att" + subordinate clause. Tense often stays the same in Swedish.

target: Hon berättade att hon hade fått jobbet.
english: She told that she had gotten the job.
grammar: Reported speech uses past perfect (hade fått) when the original was past tense (fick).

target: De sa att de skulle komma.
grammar: "Sa att" + "skulle" reports a future intention from the original speech.

target: Han frågade om jag ville följa med.
english: He asked if I wanted to come along.
grammar: Reported questions use "om" (if/whether) for yes/no questions.
Reported questions with wh-words keep the question word: var, när, hur, etc.

target: De berättade att allt hade gått bra.
english: They told that everything had gone well.
grammar: Past perfect in reported speech indicates action before the saying.

target: Han påstod att han hade rätt.
english: He claimed that he was right.
grammar: "Påstå" = claim/assert. Often implies doubt about the truth.

target: Hon meddelade att mötet var inställt.
english: She informed that the meeting was cancelled.
grammar: "Inställt" = cancelled. An adjective from "ställa in" (cancel). Past participle form.

target: De förklarade att de inte kunde komma.
english: They explained that they couldn't come.
grammar: In reported subordinate clauses, "inte" goes BEFORE the verb: "att de inte kunde."

target: Han lovade att han skulle ringa.
english: He promised that he would call.
grammar: "Skulle" in reported speech = 'would' — backshift of "ska" (will).

target: Hon hävdade att det var sant.
english: She maintained that it was true.
grammar: "Hävda" (claim/maintain) — a formal reporting verb. Everyday: "sa" (said).

target: De föreslog att vi skulle vänta.
english: They suggested that we should wait.
grammar: "Föreslå" = suggest. "Skulle" in reported speech = should/would.

target: Han erkände att han hade fel.
english: He admitted that he was wrong.
grammar: "Erkänna" = admit. "Ha fel" = be wrong. "Hade" in reported speech.

target: Hon försäkrade mig om att allt var bra.
english: She assured me that everything was fine.
grammar: "Försäkra om" = assure. "Allt" = everything. Reported past tense.

target: De varnade oss att det var farligt.
english: They warned us that it was dangerous.
grammar: "Varna" = warn. "Farligt" = dangerous (neuter with "det").

target: Han klagade över att det var kallt.
english: He complained that it was cold.

target: Hon upprepade att vi måste vara i tid.
english: She repeated that we must be on time.

target: De tillade att priset inte ingick.
english: They added that the price was not included.

target: Han nämnde att han kände henne.
english: He mentioned that he knew her.

target: Hon konstaterade att resultatet var bra.
english: She stated that the result was good.

target: De bekräftade att bokningen var klar.
english: They confirmed that the booking was ready.

target: Han invände att det var för dyrt.
english: He objected that it was too expensive.

target: Hon sa att hon inte förstod.
De menade att vi borde agera.
Han ansåg att beslutet var fel.
Hon berättade för mig att hon var sjuk.
english: She told me that she was sick.

target: De rapporterade att vädret skulle bli bättre.
english: They reported that the weather would improve.

target: Han påpekade att vi hade gjort fel.
english: He pointed out that we had made a mistake.

target: Hon avslöjade att hon var gravid.
english: She revealed that she was pregnant.

target: De informerade oss om att tåget var försenat.
english: They informed us that the train was delayed.

target: Han önskade att vi kunde stanna.
english: He wished that we could stay.

target: Hon undrade om det fanns plats.
english: She wondered if there was room.

target: De frågade när vi hade tänkt åka.
english: They asked when we had planned to leave.

target: Han ville veta hur länge vi stannade.
english: He wanted to know how long we were staying.

target: De undrade vem som hade ringt.
Han frågade varför jag var sen.
english: He asked why I was late.

target: Hon ville veta vilken film vi hade sett.
english: She wanted to know which movie we had seen.

target: De frågade hur man kom dit.
english: They asked how to get there.

target: Han undrade om butiken var öppen.
english: He wondered if the store was open.

target: Hon frågade om vi hade ätit.
english: She asked if we had eaten.

target: De ville veta om vi behövde hjälp.
english: They wanted to know if we needed help.

target: Han sa åt mig att vänta.
grammar: "Säga åt" = tell someone to do something. More forceful than "be."

target: Hon bad mig att stänga dörren.
english: She asked me to close the door.
grammar: "Be" = ask/request politely.

english: He ordered the soldiers to stay.

target: Hon föreslog att vi skulle ta en paus.
english: She suggested we take a break.

target: De rådde mig att söka jobbet.
english: They advised me to apply for the job.

target: Han krävde att vi skulle betala.
Hon övertygade mig om att det var rätt.
english: She convinced me that it was right.

target: Enligt honom var det en bra idé.
english: According to him, it was a good idea.
grammar: "Enligt" + person is another way to report: "According to..."

english: According to the newspaper, prices have increased.
grammar: "Enligt" + source is a clean way to attribute information.

target: Enligt väderprognosen ska det bli sol.
english: According to the forecast, it will be sunny.

target: I hennes ögon var det perfekt.
english: In her eyes, it was perfect.

target: Som han uttryckte det, var det en katastrof.
english: As he put it, it was a disaster.

target: Han sa i princip att han inte ville.
english: He basically said he didn't want to.

target: Hon antydde att hon tänkte sluta.
english: She hinted that she planned to quit.

target: De lät förstå att de var missnöjda.
english: They made it known they were dissatisfied.

target: Han viskade att han hade en hemlighet.
english: He whispered that he had a secret.

target: Hon skrek att vi skulle stanna.
english: She screamed that we should stay.

target: De mumlade att det var synd.
english: They mumbled that it was a pity.

target: Han suckade att det var hopplöst.
english: He sighed that it was hopeless.

target: Hon skrattade och sa att det var roligt.
english: She laughed and said it was fun.

target: De protesterade och sa att det var orättvist.
english: They protested and said it was unfair.

target: Han log och sa att allt var bra.
english: He smiled and said everything was fine.

target: Hon nickade och bekräftade att det stämde.
english: She nodded and confirmed it was correct.

target: De vinkade och ropade att vi skulle komma.
english: They waved and called that we should come.

target: Han skakade på huvudet och sa nej.
english: He shook his head and said no.

target: Nyheten sa att det var en olycka.
english: The news said it was an accident.

target: Artikeln hävdade att det var falskt.
english: The article claimed it was false.
The report showed that results had improved.

target: Studien konstaterade att det finns samband.
english: The study found that there's a connection.
The book tells that he was born in 1909.

target: Skylten sa att det var förbjudet.
english: The sign said it was forbidden.

target: Mejlet meddelade att mötet var flyttat.
english: The email informed that the meeting was moved.

target: Dokumentet fastslog att avtalet var giltigt.
english: The document established that the agreement was valid.

target: Programmet visade att mycket hade hänt.
english: The program showed that a lot had happened.

target: Jag hörde att det var en bra restaurang.
english: I heard it was a good restaurant.

target: Man säger att svensk mat är tråkig.
english: They say Swedish food is boring.
grammar: "Man säger att" = they say / it is said — used for common beliefs.

target: Det sägs att sommaren blir varm.
english: It's said the summer will be warm.
grammar: "Det sägs att" = it is said that — impersonal reported speech.

target: Det påstås att huset är hemsökt.
english: It's claimed the house is haunted.

target: Det ryktas att de ska skiljas.
grammar: "Ryktas" = be rumored. Deponent verb.

target: Det anses vara den bästa skolan.
Det uppges att situationen är allvarlig.
english: It's reported that the situation is serious.

target: Det framgår av rapporten att vi behöver mer.
english: The report shows that we need more.
It turned out that nobody knew.

target: Det visade sig att han hade rätt.
english: It turned out that he was right.

target: Det konstaterades att skadorna var små.
english: It was found that the damages were small.

target: Det beslutades att vi skulle vänta.
english: It was decided that we would wait.

target: Det överenskoms att mötet skulle hållas.
english: It was agreed that the meeting would be held.

target: Det noterades att alla var närvarande.
english: It was noted that everyone was present.

target: Det betonades att kvalitet var viktigt.
english: It was emphasized that quality was important.

target: Det rekommenderades att vi anlitade expert.
english: It was recommended that we hire an expert.

target: Han ska ha sagt att han inte visste.
english: He supposedly said he didn't know.
grammar: "Ska ha" + supine = reportedly/supposedly. Distances from the claim.

english: She is said to have told the secret.
grammar: "Lär ha" = is said to have — another way to report unconfirmed information.

target: De ska tydligen ha bestämt sig.
Enligt uppgift planeras en ny skola.
english: According to reports, a new school is planned.

target: Som jag förstod det var problemet löst.
english: As I understood it, the problem was solved.

target: Så vitt jag vet har de redan åkt.
english: As far as I know, they've already left.

target: Det är ingen ko på isen.
grammar: Literally "no cow on the ice." One of Sweden's most beloved idioms — meaning relax, everything's fine.

english: To put a lid on it.
grammar: Means to stop discussing something, to sweep it under the carpet.

target: Att gå som katten kring het gröt.
grammar: Literally "walk like a cat around hot porridge" — avoid the main point.

target: Det är ingen dans på rosor.
grammar: Literally "no dance on roses" — the situation is difficult.

target: Han tog skeden i vacker hand.
english: He got down to business graciously.
grammar: To accept a difficult situation and deal with it.

english: We were in the same boat.
grammar: Same idiom in English and Swedish — universal maritime metaphor.

target: Det rinner av som vatten på en gås.
english: Like water off a duck's back.
grammar: Literally "it runs off like water on a goose" — criticism doesn't affect them.

target: Att ha rent mjöl i påsen.
grammar: Literally "have clean flour in the bag" — nothing to hide.

target: Hon har tagit sig vatten över huvudet.
english: She has bitten off more than she can chew.
grammar: Literally "taken water over her head" — taken on too much.

target: Det faller på sin egen orimlighet.
Att göra en höna av en fjäder.
english: To make a mountain out of a molehill.
grammar: Literally "make a hen out of a feather."
"Dö" is used figuratively — "dying like a herring" means exhausted.

target: Att inte se skogen för alla träd.
english: To not see the forest for the trees.

target: Vi tar det med en nypa salt.
english: We take it with a grain of salt.
grammar: "Nypa" = pinch. Same idiom as English "grain of salt."

target: Det var droppen som fick bägaren att rinna över.
grammar: Literally "the drop that made the cup overflow."
"Gå bet" = fail at something. Colloquial.
Literally "stick out the chin" — take a risk or bold stance.

target: Det går inte att ha kakan och äta den.
english: You can't have your cake and eat it too.
She hit the nail on the head.

grammar: Literally "end up in windy weather" — get into trouble.

target: Han har ett finger med i spelet.
english: He has a finger in the pie.

target: Att inte lägga alla ägg i samma korg.
english: To not put all eggs in one basket.

target: Det är en storm i ett vattenglas.
english: It's a storm in a teacup.
She got straight to the point.
grammar: "Gå rakt på sak" = go straight to the point. Very direct.

target: Att dra alla över en kam.
english: To tar everyone with the same brush.
grammar: Literally "comb everyone the same way."

english: There are skeletons in the closet.
grammar: Literally "corpses in the wardrobe."

target: Han har svårt att hålla tungan rätt i mun.
english: He has trouble keeping his mouth shut.
grammar: Literally "hold the tongue right in the mouth" — tends to say the wrong thing.

english: To be barking up the wrong tree.
grammar: Literally "be out cycling" — be completely mistaken.
Swedes actually "hold their thumbs" (hålla tummarna) rather than cross fingers!

english: To take the bull by the horns.
She had butterflies in her stomach.

target: Det var det bästa sedan skivat bröd.
english: It was the best thing since sliced bread.

grammar: Literally "have ice in the stomach" — stay calm and patient.
Same idiom in Swedish and English — burning bridges behind you.

target: Det finns mer än ett sätt att flå en katt.
english: There's more than one way to skin a cat.

grammar: Literally "lay yourself flat" — give in unconditionally.

target: Vi satte tummen mitt i ögat på honom.
Att vara som en öppen bok.
english: To be like an open book.

grammar: Literally "costs the shirt" — extremely expensive.

target: Han har bränt sig på den soppan förut.
english: He's been burned by that before.
We took advantage of the situation.
grammar: "Dra nytta av" = benefit from. Very common business expression.

english: To pour oil on the fire.

grammar: "Ta det med ro" = take it calmly. "Ro" = peace/calm.

english: It was like it had vanished into thin air.

target: Att stå med ryggen mot väggen.
english: To have one's back against the wall.
We got a taste for it.
grammar: Literally "got blood on the tooth" — became eager to continue.

target: Att vara het som en gryta.
grammar: Literally "hot as a pot" — something everyone wants.

target: Det ligger i linje med planen.
english: It is in line with the plan.
To know which way the wind blows.
He shed light on the matter.

grammar: Means so bad it can't even be criticized properly.

target: Hon hade en räv bakom örat.
grammar: Literally "had a fox behind the ear" — was clever/sly.

target: Att ge sig i kast med något.
Att vara en nagel i ögat.
english: To be a thorn in one's side.

target: Hon levde ur hand i mun.
english: She lived from hand to mouth.
We put our finger on the problem.

grammar: Literally "went like greased" — smoothly and effortlessly.

english: To get back on one's feet.

target: Han hade tummen mitt i handen.
grammar: Literally "had the thumb in the middle of the hand" — clumsy.

target: Vi lät gräset växa under fötterna.
english: We let the grass grow under our feet.

target: Det var en sak för sig.
english: That was a whole different matter.

grammar: Literally "take the leaf from the mouth" — be direct/candid.

target: Vi gick med på deras villkor.

english: To stand on one's own two feet.
We put an end to the discussion.

grammar: Literally "went to the forest" — completely failed.

english: To have a handle on the situation.
She got a foot in the door.

target: Det var som att slå i en dörr.
english: It was like talking to a brick wall.
We took a running start and jumped.

grammar: "I det blå" means unknown, uncertain — it's all up in the blue.

target: Vi kom överens på stående fot.
Att dra lärdom av sina misstag.
Hon tog saken i egna händer.
english: She took matters into her own hands.

grammar: "Skynda sig långsamt" = hurry slowly — a very Swedish approach to getting things done.
Literally "a narrow thing" — a piece of cake.

target: Att visa var skåpet ska stå.
grammar: Literally "show where the cupboard should stand" — assert authority.

english: We put our cards on the table.
grammar: "Lägga korten på bordet" = be transparent about everything.

target: Hon fick vatten på sin kvarn.
english: She got grist for her mill.
To keep a sharp eye on.
grammar: Literally "eagle surveillance" — very attentive monitoring.

target: Han la sig vinn om att vara artig.
english: He made an effort to be polite.

grammar: Swedes "hold their thumbs" rather than cross fingers — the Swedish good luck gesture.
Literally "stayed on the carpet" — stayed in line, behaved.

english: We got wind of the matter.

grammar: "Tappa sugen" = lose one's enthusiasm/drive.

english: We pulled in the same direction.

grammar: Means eager to do something.
"Skulle du kunna" is more polite than "Kan du." Adds social distance and respect.
Casual and direct — perfectly fine among friends and peers.

target: Jag undrar om ni har tid.
english: I wonder if you have time.
grammar: "Ni" as polite "you" is making a small comeback in customer service contexts.
"Skulle vilja" = would like. The polite standard in restaurants and shops.
Direct "jag vill ha" is casual but common among friends.
Formal/polite opener before asking a stranger something.
Casual opener — very common in everyday Swedish.

target: Jag ber om ursäkt för förseningen.
grammar: Formal apology. Used in business or official settings.
Casual apology among friends.
Very formal thanks. Rarely used in everyday speech.
Casual and warm.
Standard formal email/letter closing in Swedish.
Casual email closing. "Mvh" = "med vänliga hälsningar" abbreviated.

target: Vi ber er vänligen att vänta.
english: We kindly ask you to wait.
grammar: Formal/institutional language.

target: Det är med stor glädje jag meddelar.
english: It is with great pleasure I announce.
grammar: Very formal announcement style.

english: Check it out, I got the job!
grammar: Very casual exclamation.
Formal written Swedish, typical in official correspondence.
Standard casual/neutral tone.
Very formal — seen in official notices.

target: Jag vore tacksam om ni kunde.
english: I would be grateful if you could.
grammar: "Vore" is subjunctive of "vara" — very polite/formal.

target: Jag hade blivit glad om du kunde.
english: I'd be happy if you could.
grammar: Casual conditional request.
"Torde" = presumably should. Very formal/written Swedish.

target: Jag önskar er en trevlig dag.
english: I wish you a pleasant day.

grammar: Formal bureaucratic language.
Very formal conditional — business/official.

target: Det åligger var och en att följa reglerna.
english: It is incumbent upon everyone to follow the rules.
grammar: "Åligger" = it is the duty of. Very formal/legal.

target: Jag vill framföra mitt djupaste tack.
english: I wish to convey my deepest thanks.

grammar: Casual and enthusiastic. "Jätte-" prefix adds emphasis.

target: Var god och fyll i formuläret.
grammar: "Var god" = please. Standard in official instructions.
Very formal legal/official language.

english: We respectfully request the matter be handled.
grammar: "Hemställa" = formally request. Used in official/governmental contexts.

target: Vi vill att det här fixas.
english: We want this to be fixed.

grammar: Very formal opening for speeches.

target: Jag vänder mig till er med en fråga.
english: I turn to you with a question.
grammar: Formal way to address someone with a request.
"Fråga" is both a noun (en fråga) and a verb (att fråga). Very common.

english: It would have been desirable if.
grammar: Formal conditional — business/academic.
Casual — using English loanword "nice" is very common in spoken Swedish.

english: The person in question has not responded.
grammar: "Vederbörande" = the person in question. Very formal.
Military/very formal bureaucratic style.

english: I want to take time off.
We ask to get back to you.
grammar: Formal customer service language.
Casual. Very common way to end a conversation.

english: How would you like to pay?
grammar: Formal customer service Swedish.

english: How do you want to pay?

target: Det förefaller som om vi har ett problem.
english: It appears as if we have a problem.
grammar: "Förefalla" = to appear/seem. Very formal.

target: Det verkar som vi har ett problem.
english: It seems like we have a problem.

target: Tack för att ni hjälpte till!
Jag ber om att få framföra.
english: I ask to be allowed to present.

grammar: "Emotse" = formally await. Used in business letters.

target: Jag önskar er en angenäm helg.
english: I wish you a pleasant weekend.
I take the liberty of reminding.
We would be deeply obliged to you.
grammar: Extremely formal gratitude. Almost never heard in speech.

english: With your permission I would like to emphasize.
I want to point out that.

grammar: Standard formal apology in customer service.
Formal — used in official memos.

english: Furthermore, we wish to inform about.

target: Och sen vill vi berätta att.
english: And then we want to tell you that.

target: Tack för att du litar på mig.
Du och jag, vi fixar det.
english: You and me, we'll fix it.
grammar: The familiarity and directness of Swedish informal register — "du" with everyone.

target: Vi får se till att lösa detta skyndsamt.
english: We must see to it that this is resolved promptly.
I have the honor to convey.

grammar: Particle verbs: verb + particle (upp, ner, ut, in, av, på). The particle changes the meaning.
"Stänga av" = turn off. "Stänga" alone = close.
"Sätta på" = turn on. Opposite of "stänga av."
"Ta av sig" = take off (clothing). Reflexive particle verb.
"Gå ut" = go out. "Gå in" = go in.

english: He looks forward to the vacation.
grammar: "Se fram emot" = look forward to. The particle "fram emot" is two words.
"Ge upp" = give up.
"Bryta ihop" = break down. "Ihop" = together/apart.

target: Hon slår upp ett ord i ordboken.
english: She looks up a word in the dictionary.

grammar: "Ta reda på" = find out / investigate.
"Hålla med" = agree. The particle "med" changes the meaning from "hold."

target: Hon lägger ner tid på det.
grammar: "Lägga ner" = put down/invest (time/effort). Also = shut down (a business).

target: Han ger sig av klockan sju.
grammar: "Ge sig av" = leave/set off. Very common.
"Hålla på med" = be busy with / be in the process of.
"Sticka iväg" = leave/head off. Casual.
"Dyka upp" = show up/appear.
"Ta emot" = receive/accept.
"Ställa in" = cancel. Very important particle verb.
"Skjuta upp" = postpone.
"Kasta bort" = throw away.

target: Han bryter av mitt i meningen.

grammar: "Hinna med" = manage to keep up with / have time for.

target: Hon slår sig ner i soffan.
english: She sits down on the sofa.
grammar: "Slå sig ner" = sit down / settle in.
"Stiga på" = get on (transport). "Stiga av" = get off.
"Koppla av" = relax/unwind. Literally "disconnect."

english: She tidies up after the party.

grammar: "Fylla på" = refill/top up.
"Komma överens" = agree / come to an agreement.
"Gå igenom" = go through / review.

english: She takes care of the children.
grammar: "Ta hand om" = take care of. Extremely common.

target: Vi ger oss av på resa.
english: We set off on a trip.

grammar: "Hitta på" = make up / invent. Also = come up with an activity.
"Lägga ihop" = fold / put together.
"Räkna ut" = calculate / figure out.
"Se efter" = look after / keep an eye on.
"Sätta igång" = start up / get going.
"Hälla upp" = pour (a drink).
"Lägga till" = add.
"Dra sig tillbaka" = withdraw / retire.

target: Jag sätter mig in i ämnet.
english: I familiarize myself with the subject.
grammar: "Sätta sig in i" = familiarize oneself with.
"Ställa om" = reset / adjust.
"Ta upp" = bring up / raise (a topic).
"Lägga av" = quit / stop. Colloquial.

english: She stands out from the crowd.

grammar: "Ta sig an" = take on / tackle.
"Lyfta fram" = highlight / bring forward.

english: She grits her teeth and fights.

target: Vi går vidare till nästa punkt.
english: We move on to the next point.
grammar: "Gå vidare" = move on / proceed.
"Ta bort" = remove / take away.
"Torka av" = wipe off/clean.
"Sätta upp" = put up / hang up.
"Knyta ihop säcken" = wrap things up / bring to conclusion. Literally "tie up the sack."
"Skjuta till" = contribute / chip in.
"Driva på" = push / drive forward.
"Springa om" = overtake (while running).
"Slå sig lös" = let loose / cut loose.
"Hänga med" = follow along / understand. Also = come along.
"Räcka till" = be sufficient / be enough.
"Hålla ut" = endure / persevere.

target: Vi bär oss åt som vuxna.
grammar: "Bära sig åt" = behave.
"Ge sig" = give in / surrender.
"Skärpa sig" = pull oneself together / shape up.
"Tappa bort" = lose (an object).
"Klara av" = manage / handle.

english: He puts up with the pain.
grammar: "Stå ut med" = put up with / endure.

target: Jag ser upp till min pappa.
english: I look up to my dad.
grammar: "Se upp till" = look up to / admire.

target: Hon ser till att allt är klart.
english: She makes sure everything is ready.
grammar: "Se till att" = make sure that.

target: Det var igår som jag träffade henne.
english: It was yesterday that I met her.
grammar: Cleft sentence: "Det var ... som" focuses on a particular element for emphasis.
"Det är DU som bestämmer" emphasizes who. Very common pattern.

target: Det är i Stockholm som hon bor.
english: It is in Stockholm that she lives.
grammar: Cleft sentence: 'Det är X som...' emphasizes X. Very common in spoken Swedish.

target: Aldrig förr hade jag sett något sådant.
english: Never before had I seen anything like it.
grammar: Fronting a negative adverb creates a very emphatic, literary construction.

target: Inte ens en gång tänkte han på det.
english: Not even once did he think about it.
grammar: Fronted negation triggers V2: "Inte ens..." + verb + subject. Dramatic emphasis.

target: Sällan har jag ätit så gott.
english: Seldom have I eaten so well.
grammar: Fronted adverbs trigger inversion: Sällan + har + jag. Adds emphasis and literary flavor.

target: Knappt hade vi anlänt förrän det började regna.
english: Hardly had we arrived when it started raining.
grammar: "Knappt...förrän" = barely...when. Triggers inversion and uses past perfect.

target: Inte bara regnade det, det blåste också.
english: Not only did it rain, it was also windy.
grammar: "Inte bara...också" (not only...also). First clause inverts; second is normal order.

target: Vad jag tycker spelar ingen roll.
grammar: Subordinate clause as subject — placed first with no inversion in the main clause.

target: Att lära sig ett nytt språk tar tid.
english: Learning a new language takes time.
grammar: "Att" + infinitive clause as subject.

target: Hur vi löser problemet vet jag inte.
english: How we solve the problem I don't know.
grammar: Fronted indirect question as topic: "Hur vi löser" + inverted main: "vet jag inte."

target: Var hon bor är en hemlighet.
english: Where she lives is a secret.
grammar: Indirect question as subject: "Var hon bor" = where she lives.
Indirect question as subject. Normal word order inside: "vad han sa."
"Att"-clause as subject. "Förvånade" = surprised. Very natural in Swedish.

target: Duktig som hon är löser hon allt.
english: Skilled as she is, she solves everything.
grammar: Adjective fronting for emphasis: adjective + som + subject + verb.

target: Trött som jag var somnade jag direkt.
english: Tired as I was, I fell asleep right away.

target: Vacker som dagen var gick vi ut.
english: Beautiful as the day was, we went out.

grammar: Verb topicalization: placing the infinitive first for emphasis.

english: Run I can't be bothered to.

grammar: Adverb topicalization with V2 inversion.

target: Det var kaffe hon ville ha, inte te.
english: It was coffee she wanted, not tea.

target: Det var förra veckan som vi träffades.
english: It was last week that we met.

target: Det var därför vi inte kom.
english: That was why we didn't come.

target: Så sa han det, men jag trodde inte på honom.
english: So he said, but I didn't believe him.

grammar: "Det + göra" as pro-verb for emphasis: "She really DID."

target: Gå kan jag, men inte springa.
english: Walk I can, but not run.

target: Pengar har vi inte, men tid har vi.
english: Money we don't have, but time we do.
grammar: Parallel fronting creates contrast between two objects.

target: I Sverige bor det tio miljoner människor.
english: In Sweden live ten million people.
Under the table lay the cat.
In the middle of the night the phone rang.
Nowhere could I find the key.

target: Så fort jag kom hem somnade jag.
english: As soon as I got home I fell asleep.

target: Inte förrän nästa vecka kan jag svara.
english: Not until next week can I answer.

target: Bara i Sverige finns denna tradition.
english: Only in Sweden does this tradition exist.

grammar: Exclamatory word order: "Vad" + adjective + subject + verb.

target: Så trött var han att han somnade.
english: So tired was he that he fell asleep.

target: Liten som han var kunde han ändå hjälpa.
english: Small as he was, he could still help.

target: Den boken har jag redan läst.
english: That book I have already read.
grammar: Object topicalization — placing the object first for emphasis.

english: Those words I will never forget.

target: Det här problemet måste vi lösa.

english: You I want to talk to.

grammar: Object fronting for contrast: "KAFFE dricker jag svart" (not tea).

target: Till dig har jag en present.
english: For you I have a present.
More time we would have needed.

target: Annorlunda hade det inte kunnat bli.
Sedan dess har jag inte sett henne.
english: Since then I haven't seen her.

target: Då och då tänker jag på det.
english: Now and then I think about it.

target: Ännu idag minns jag den kvällen.
english: Even today I remember that evening.

target: Steg för steg lärde hon sig.
Lite i taget blev det bättre.
english: Little by little it got better.
More often than not it rains.

target: Mig veterligen finns det inget bevis.
english: To my knowledge there's no evidence.
grammar: "Mig veterligen" = to my knowledge. Formal/literary.

target: Så vitt jag vet stämmer det.
english: As far as I know, it's correct.
That I consider to be incorrect.

target: Mitt bland alla gäster stod hon.
english: In the middle of all the guests she stood.
From this one can draw the conclusion.
I hereby declare the meeting closed.
Before that we had eaten dinner.
grammar: "Dessförinnan" = before that. Formal connector.

english: Beyond that there are other advantages.

target: Var vi än går följer naturen.
Hur svårt det än är ger jag inte upp.
english: No matter how hard it is, I won't give up.

target: Vem som än frågar, säg inget.
När som helst kan det hända.
english: It can happen at any time.

target: Var som helst i Sverige hittar man sjöar.
english: Anywhere in Sweden you find lakes.

target: Hur du än gör blir det fel.
english: No matter what you do, it'll be wrong.

target: Vad du än säger lyssnar hon inte.
english: Whatever you say, she doesn't listen.
That I don't know at all.

grammar: "Inte heller" = neither/nor. "Inte heller jag" = me neither.

target: Enbart genom samarbete kan vi lyckas.
english: Only through cooperation can we succeed.

target: Det var inte alls vad jag förväntade mig.
english: It was not at all what I expected.

target: Inte heller i Stockholm har jag bott.
english: Neither in Stockholm have I lived.

target: Det var med stor förvåning jag hörde.
english: It was with great surprise I heard.
Only together can we manage it.
All at once I understood everything.
At long last the news came.

target: Allt emellanåt tänker jag på henne.
english: Every now and then I think about her.
Above all I appreciate the honesty.

target: I grund och botten har vi samma åsikt.
english: Fundamentally we have the same opinion.

target: Lång tid har gått sedan dess.
english: A long time has passed since then.

target: Allt jag vill ha är lugn och ro.
english: All I want is peace and quiet.

target: Det enda vi kan göra är att vänta.
english: The only thing we can do is wait.
All that remained was the chair.

target: Inte på länge har jag känt mig så bra.
english: Not in a long time have I felt this good.

target: Så bra har det aldrig varit.
english: It has never been this good.

target: Den mörka natten sänkte sig över landskapet.
english: The dark night descended upon the landscape.
grammar: Literary style uses more descriptive, poetic language with longer constructions.

english: The forest stood quiet and still.
grammar: Literary Swedish often personifies nature — the forest "stands" as if alive.

target: En blek måne lyste genom molnen.
english: A pale moon shone through the clouds.
grammar: "Lysa" = shine. "Blek" = pale. Poetic vocabulary for nature writing.

target: Dimman lade sig som ett täcke.
english: The fog settled like a blanket.
grammar: Similes with "som" (like/as): "som ett täcke" = like a blanket.
"Öronbedövande" = ear-deafening. Swedish builds vivid compounds: noun + participle.

target: Han vandrade ensam genom den töckenhöljda dalen.
english: He walked alone through the mist-shrouded valley.
grammar: "Töckenhöljd" = mist-covered. Literary compound: töcken (mist) + höljd (covered).

target: Orden fastnade som taggar i minnet.
english: The words stuck like thorns in memory.
grammar: "Fastna" (get stuck) — a vivid verb for things that linger. "Taggar" = thorns.

target: Hennes ögon avslöjade det hon inte sa.
english: Her eyes revealed what she didn't say.
grammar: "Avslöja" = reveal/betray. Literary usage: eyes revealing hidden emotions.

english: Slowly the city awoke to life.
grammar: Adverb fronting in literary style creates atmosphere and rhythm.

target: Vintermörkret svepte in som ett svart skynke.
english: The winter darkness swept in like a black curtain.
grammar: "Svepa in" = sweep in. "Skynke" = cloth/shroud — poetic word, rare in everyday speech.

target: Aldrig hade känslan av ensamhet känts så starkt.
english: Never had the feeling of loneliness felt so strong.
grammar: Fronted "aldrig" + inverted word order = literary emphasis. "Kännas" = feel (s-verb).

target: I fjärran hördes ljudet av bjällror.
english: In the distance the sound of bells was heard.
grammar: "I fjärran" = in the distance (literary). "Hördes" = was heard (s-passive).

target: Det var som om tiden stod stilla.
english: It was as if time stood still.
grammar: "Som om" = as if. Creates hypothetical comparison.

target: Löven föll sakta mot den frusna marken.
english: The leaves fell slowly toward the frozen ground.
grammar: "Frusen" = frozen. Past participle of "frysa" used as adjective.

target: Han bar med sig en hemlighet som tyngde.
english: He carried with him a secret that weighed heavy.
grammar: "Bära med sig" = carry with oneself. "Tynga" = weigh down — metaphorical usage.

target: Ljuset sipprade in genom de smutsiga fönstren.
english: Light seeped in through the dirty windows.
grammar: "Sippra" = seep/trickle. Literary verbs paint vivid pictures of slow movement.

target: Deras blickar möttes över det dukade bordet.
english: Their eyes met across the set table.
grammar: "Mötas" = meet (reciprocal s-verb). "Dukat bord" = set table. Romantic imagery.

target: Minnet av sommarens dagar värmde hennes hjärta.
english: The memory of summer days warmed her heart.
grammar: "Sommarens" = summer's (genitive -s). Possessive -s works like English apostrophe-s.

english: The bells in the church tower struck twelve.
grammar: "Slå" → "slog" (struck). "Kyrktorn" = church tower. Literary time-marking device.

english: The snow crunched under his boots.
grammar: "Gnissla" = crunch/squeak. Onomatopoeia — Swedish has many such expressive verbs.

target: Strömmen flöt sakta genom den sovande byn.
english: The stream flowed slowly through the sleeping village.
grammar: "Sovande" = sleeping (present participle). Personification of the village.

target: Hösten hade klätt landskapet i guld.
english: Autumn had dressed the landscape in gold.
grammar: "Klä" = dress/clothe. "Klä i" = dress in. Metaphor for autumn colors.

target: En känsla av vemod fyllde hennes bröst.
english: A feeling of melancholy filled her chest.
grammar: "Vemod" is a uniquely Scandinavian form of bittersweet sadness.

target: Han lutade sig mot stammen av den gamla eken.
english: He leaned against the trunk of the old oak.
grammar: "Luta sig" = lean. "Stam" = trunk. "Ek" = oak (en ek, eken).

target: Tidvattnet bar med sig vrakdelar från det förflutna.
english: The tide carried with it wreckage from the past.
grammar: "Det förflutna" = the past (literary). "Vrakdelar" = wreckage (compound).

target: Skuggorna dansade i ljuset av brasan.
english: The shadows danced in the light of the fire.

target: Det var en tid som aldrig skulle återvända.
english: It was a time that would never return.

target: Hennes röst bar spår av smärta.
english: Her voice carried traces of pain.
The wind whispered secrets through the birches.

target: Den förbipasserande skogsduvan kutade genom dimman.
english: The passing wood pigeon cooed through the fog.
The years passed, but the memories endured.
grammar: "Bestå" = endure/persist. Literary vocabulary for permanence.

target: I mörkret sken stjärnorna som diamanter.
english: In the darkness the stars shone like diamonds.

target: Han begrundade sitt öde under de tysta stjärnorna.
english: He pondered his fate beneath the silent stars.
The morning dew glistened on the blades of grass.

target: En ensam fågel sjöng sin sista visa.
english: A solitary bird sang its last song.
grammar: "Visa" = song (poetic). "Sång" is the everyday word.

target: Skymningen bredde ut sig som en mjuk filt.
english: Twilight spread out like a soft blanket.

target: Havets röst var en ständig följeslagare.
english: The voice of the sea was a constant companion.

target: Bergstoppen stod klar mot den blånande himlen.
english: The mountain peak stood clear against the blue sky.

target: Doften av nyslaget hö fyllde luften.
english: The scent of freshly cut hay filled the air.

target: En väg, knappt synlig, ledde in bland träden.
english: A road, barely visible, led in among the trees.

target: Huset hade stått tomt sedan lång tid tillbaka.
english: The house had stood empty for a long time.

target: Skeppet gled tyst genom den spegelblanka fjorden.
english: The ship glided silently through the mirror-smooth fjord.

target: Den gamla mannens ögon berättade mer än ord.
english: The old man's eyes told more than words.

target: Sommarnattens ljus vägrade ge vika för mörkret.
english: The summer night's light refused to yield to darkness.

target: Inunder den lugna ytan doldes en storm.
english: Beneath the calm surface a storm was hidden.

target: Varje steg på den knarrande trappan ljöd.
english: Every step on the creaking staircase sounded.

target: I det avlägsna förflutna hade allt varit annorlunda.
english: In the distant past everything had been different.

target: Den tomma stolen vid bordet talade sitt tysta språk.
english: The empty chair at the table spoke its silent language.

target: Natten svalde de sista resterna av dagsljuset.
english: The night swallowed the last remnants of daylight.
The shoreline was bordered by frozen wave crests.
Horses grazed peacefully in the meadow.

target: Det gamla hjulet knarrade i vinden.
english: The old wheel creaked in the wind.

target: Solnedgången målade himlen i alla nyanser av rött.
english: The sunset painted the sky in all shades of red.

target: Horisonten sträckte sig oändligt framför dem.
english: The horizon stretched endlessly before them.
The ice crackled under their feet.

target: Fjällen reste sig majestätiskt mot skyarna.
english: The mountains rose majestically toward the skies.
A veil of mist drifted across the lake.

target: Sorgen hade etsats in i hans anletsdrag.
english: Grief had been etched into his features.

target: Varje dröm bär fröet till sin egen fullbordan.
english: Every dream carries the seed of its own fulfillment.
The tireless progress of the tide continued.

target: Skogens djup dolde hemligheter bortom fattning.
english: The depths of the forest hid secrets beyond comprehension.

target: Med ens steg en gestalt fram ur skuggorna.
english: Suddenly a figure stepped out from the shadows.

target: Skönheten i det förgängliga fångade hans blick.
english: The beauty in the transient caught his eye.
The deciduous trees rustled in the evening breeze.

target: Deras öde var beseglat av det sagda.
english: Their fate was sealed by what was said.

target: Ljusets dans på vattenytan skapade rörliga mönster.
english: The dance of light on the water surface created moving patterns.

target: Den stormiga havsytan speglade hans inre oro.
english: The stormy sea surface mirrored his inner unrest.
The snowflakes swirled like dancing spirits.

target: I nattens stillhet hördes bara ugglans rop.
english: In the stillness of night, only the owl's call was heard.

target: Den urgamla sagan har berättats i generationer.
english: The ancient tale has been told for generations.
The recluse had chosen the forest's solitude.
Spring arrived with the scent of promise.

target: I gryningens ljus tedde sig allt annorlunda.
english: In the light of dawn, everything appeared different.

target: Tystnaden efter stormen var nästan hörbar.
english: The silence after the storm was almost audible.
The river of memories never runs dry.

target: Det bleka ljuset kastade långa skuggor.
english: The pale light cast long shadows.

target: Tidens tand hade nött på de gamla murarna.
english: The tooth of time had worn on the old walls.

target: Under midnattssolen förlorade tiden sin mening.
english: Under the midnight sun, time lost its meaning.
grammar: The midnight sun in northern Sweden means the sun never sets during summer.

target: En svag rökpelare steg från stugan.
english: A faint column of smoke rose from the cabin.

target: Sagornas land låg gömt bland dimhöljda berg.
english: The land of tales lay hidden among mist-shrouded mountains.
The horizon dazzled with the midwinter day's light.

target: Den sista rosenknoppade innan frosten kom.
english: The last rose budded before the frost came.
The sea whispered its ancient tales.

target: Kylan bet i kinderna som tusen nålar.
english: The cold bit the cheeks like a thousand needles.

target: Förgängelsens andetag vilade över den förfallna herrgården.
english: The breath of decay rested over the dilapidated manor.

target: Blickarna talade där orden inte räckte till.
english: The glances spoke where words fell short.

target: En svala flög lågt — ett tecken på regn.
english: A swallow flew low — a sign of rain.

target: I bokens gulnade sidor fann hon ett pressblad.
english: In the book's yellowed pages she found a pressed leaf.

target: Stugans väggar bar spåren av hundra vintrar.
english: The cabin's walls bore the marks of a hundred winters.

target: Hjärtats melodi klang tyst under allting.
english: The heart's melody rang quietly beneath everything.

target: Hans fotsteg ekade genom den tomma salen.
english: His footsteps echoed through the empty hall.

target: Tiden läker alla sår, så sägs det.
english: Time heals all wounds, so they say.
Nature rested in its winter slumber.

target: Den sista bladen föll som gyllene tårar.
english: The last leaves fell like golden tears.

target: Grynigen steg sakta ur havets famn.
english: Dawn rose slowly from the embrace of the sea.

target: Bakom varje horisont väntar en ny.
english: Behind every horizon awaits a new one.

target: De stilla vattnen dolde djupa strömmar.
english: The still waters hid deep currents.
Under the northern lights the world stood still.

target: I regnets trumvirvel fann hon tröst.
english: In the drumroll of rain she found comfort.

target: Förtrollningen låg i det som inte sades.
english: The enchantment lay in what was not said.

target: Årstidernas kretslopp är livets eviga dans.
english: The cycle of seasons is life's eternal dance.

target: De tvekade vid vägskälet — valet tyngde.
english: They hesitated at the crossroads — the choice weighed heavy.
Where the paths cross, fates meet.

target: Sången dog ut i den svala kvällsluften.
english: The song died out in the cool evening air.

target: Den gamle berättaren log och började.
english: The old storyteller smiled and began.

target: Studien visar att det finns ett samband.
english: The study shows that there is a connection.
grammar: Academic Swedish uses precise, impersonal constructions and hedging language.
"Tyder på" = indicates/suggests — common hedging in academic Swedish.

target: Enligt tidigare studier är sambandet tydligt.
english: According to previous studies, the connection is clear.
grammar: "Enligt" = according to. Standard citation format in academic Swedish.

target: Syftet med denna uppsats är att undersöka.
english: The purpose of this essay is to examine.
grammar: "Denna" (this) is formal. Everyday Swedish uses "den här."
"Huruvida" = whether (formal). Everyday Swedish uses "om" instead.

english: The results point in the same direction.
grammar: "Peka i riktning" = point in direction. Academic hedging language.

target: Det kan konstateras att hypotesen stämmer.
english: It can be established that the hypothesis is correct.
grammar: "Det kan konstateras" — impersonal passive, very common in Swedish academic writing.

english: Further research is required in the field.
grammar: "Krävas" = be required. Impersonal passive very common in academic writing.

english: The method is based on qualitative interviews.
grammar: "Bygga på" = be based on. Academic: "kvalitativ" (qualitative) vs. "kvantitativ" (quantitative).

target: Data samlades in under en tremånadersperiod.
english: Data was collected during a three-month period.
grammar: "Samla in" = collect. Academic Swedish favors passive constructions.

target: Analysen genomfördes med hjälp av statistisk programvara.
english: The analysis was conducted using statistical software.
grammar: "Med hjälp av" = with the help of / using. Standard academic phrasing.

english: The sample consisted of one hundred participants.
grammar: "Bestå av" = consist of. "Urval" = selection/sample — key research terminology.

english: The results are presented in table three.
grammar: "Presenteras" — s-passive is dominant in Swedish academic writing. Impersonal style.

english: The discussion revolves around three main themes.
grammar: "Kretsa kring" = revolve around. "Huvudtema" = main theme (compound).

target: Slutsatsen är att det behövs fler studier.
english: The conclusion is that more studies are needed.
grammar: "Behövas" = be needed (passive). Standard closing for academic papers.

english: The theoretical framework is based on.
grammar: "Baseras på" = is based on (s-passive). "Ramverk" = framework.

english: The material has been processed qualitatively.
grammar: "Bearbeta" = process/analyze. Past s-passive: "har bearbetats."

english: The respondents' answers were coded thematically.
grammar: "Respondenternas" = the respondents' (double genitive). "-erna" (def.) + "-s" (genitive).

english: The validity of the study can be questioned.
grammar: "Ifrågasätta" = question/challenge. Essential critical thinking verb.

english: The reliability is assessed as high.
grammar: "Bedöma" = assess/judge. "Bedöms som" = is assessed as — standard academic phrasing.
"Signifikant" = significant (statistical term). Same word as English, Swedish pronunciation.

english: The correlation between the variables is strong.
grammar: "Variablerna" = the variables (def. plural). Latin-origin words common in academic Swedish.

english: The hypothesis could not be falsified.
grammar: "Falsifiera" = falsify. "Kunde inte falsifieras" = could not be falsified.

english: The limitations of the study should be mentioned.
grammar: "Begränsning" = limitation. "Bör" = should (formal). "Nämnas" = be mentioned.

target: I denna kontext avses med begreppet.
english: In this context, the concept refers to.
grammar: "Avses" = is meant (s-passive of "avse"). "Begrepp" = concept.
"Kunskapslucka" = knowledge gap — essential academic vocabulary.

english: The results are consistent with previous research.
grammar: "Överensstämma med" = correspond with/agree with. Formal academic verb.

english: The empirical evidence supports the proposed theory.

target: Vi kan med viss försiktighet hävda.
grammar: "Med viss försiktighet" = with some caution — academic hedging.

target: Det torde vara rimligt att anta.
english: It should be reasonable to assume.
The study contributes to the research field through.
Based on the collected data, it is apparent.
The tendency is clear in the material.
The findings have implications for practice.

target: Fenomenet har studerats ur olika perspektiv.
english: The phenomenon has been studied from different perspectives.
grammar: "Ur" = from (formal). "Olika perspektiv" = various perspectives.

target: Argumentet grundar sig på empiriska data.
english: The argument is based on empirical data.
Factors that affect the result include.
The research to date has shown.
There seems to be a paradox.
The study takes its starting point in.
Against the background of these results.
The debate on the issue is multifaceted.
The theories have developed in parallel.
The social context should be taken into account.
The issue is complex and multidimensional.
The concepts require a clearer definition.

target: Tolkningen av data medger flera möjligheter.
english: The interpretation of data allows several possibilities.

target: Vi bör vara försiktiga med generaliseringar.
english: We should be cautious with generalizations.
The investigation was based on a survey study.

target: Den etnografiska metoden gav djupare insikter.
english: The ethnographic method provided deeper insights.

target: Begreppet har definierats på olika sätt.
english: The concept has been defined in different ways.
The perspective enables a broader understanding.

target: Kontextuella faktorer spelar en avgörande roll.
english: Contextual factors play a decisive role.
The pattern is consistent throughout the material.
The connection needs to be studied more closely.
The variation can be explained by.
Intersectionality is a central analytical framework.
Ethical considerations have been taken into account.

target: Informerat samtycke inhämtades från alla deltagare.
english: Informed consent was obtained from all participants.
The study follows the Swedish Research Council's ethical principles.

target: Det existerar en spänning mellan teori och praktik.
english: There exists a tension between theory and practice.
The discourse is characterized by conflicting perspectives.
The dissertation deals with the question of.
The causal relationship has not been established.
The control group showed similar results.
The effect of the intervention was measured.
The cross-sectional study covered five regions.
The collection method included surveys and interviews.
The response rate amounted to 72 percent.
The dropout analysis showed no systematic deviations.

target: Den statistiska analysen gjordes i SPSS.
english: The statistical analysis was done in SPSS.
The significance level was set at p < 0.05.
The dependent variable was clearly affected.
A thematic analysis of the interview material.
Codes and categories were developed inductively.

target: Triangulering användes för att stärka validiteten.
english: Triangulation was used to strengthen validity.
The study's relevance is motivated by.

target: Det praktiska bidraget rör sig om.

english: Finally, it should be mentioned that.

target: Lagom är bäst, brukar man säga.
english: Just the right amount is best, as they say.
grammar: "Lagom" is Sweden's unofficial motto — not too much, not too little.

target: Allemansrätten ger alla tillgång till naturen.
english: The right of public access gives everyone access to nature.
grammar: Allemansrätten is unique to Sweden — you can hike, camp, and pick berries on any land.

target: Fikan är en helig institution i Sverige.
english: Fika is a sacred institution in Sweden.
grammar: Fika isn't just coffee — it's a social ritual, a mandatory break, a way of life.

target: Midsommar firas med dans runt stången.
english: Midsummer is celebrated with dancing around the maypole.

target: Personnumret är centralt i svensk vardag.
english: The personal ID number is central in Swedish daily life.
grammar: Every resident gets a personnummer — used for healthcare, banking, everything.

target: Jantelagen säger att man inte ska tro att man är bättre.
english: The Law of Jante says you shouldn't think you're better.
grammar: Jantelagen is the unspoken social code against boasting — deeply embedded in Nordic culture.

english: Cozy Friday means tacos and TV.
grammar: Fredagsmys is a uniquely Swedish tradition — relaxing on Friday with comfort food.

target: Vi tar av oss skorna när vi går in.
english: We take off our shoes when we go inside.
grammar: One of the strictest unwritten rules in Swedish homes.

english: Systembolaget has a monopoly on alcohol.
grammar: State-run liquor stores — you can't buy wine/spirits in supermarkets.
White-robed processions with candles, singing "Sankta Lucia" — magical tradition.

target: Allemansrätten gäller inte för jakt och fiske.
english: The right of public access doesn't apply to hunting and fishing.
Child allowance is paid out every month.
grammar: Sweden's generous child benefits are a cornerstone of the welfare state.

target: Förskolan är nästan gratis i Sverige.
english: Preschool is almost free in Sweden.
Parental leave is often shared between parents.
grammar: Sweden offers 480 days of parental leave, with 90 days reserved for each parent.

english: Gender equality is a central value.
Walpurgis Night is celebrated on the last of April.
grammar: Huge bonfires and singing welcome spring — especially popular among students.

english: The crayfish party is held in August.
grammar: Paper hats, singing snapsvisor, and eating crayfish under paper lanterns.

target: Vi äter semlor från januari till påsk.
english: We eat semlor from January to Easter.
grammar: Semla season is serious business — cream-filled buns with almond paste.

target: Nobelpriset delas ut den tionde december.
english: The Nobel Prize is awarded on December 10th.

grammar: Sweden recycles so effectively it imports garbage from other countries for energy.

english: A cashless society is almost a reality.
grammar: Many Swedish businesses no longer accept cash — only card and Swish.

target: Stuglivet är en del av den svenska identiteten.
english: Cabin life is part of Swedish identity.
grammar: Many Swedes have a sommarstuga (summer cabin) in the countryside.

english: The cherry blossoms in Kungsträdgården are popular.

target: Den svenska flaggan hissas på flaggdagar.
english: The Swedish flag is raised on flag days.
Christmas Eve is the main celebration day.
grammar: Unlike many countries, Swedes celebrate Christmas on the 24th, not the 25th.

target: Kalle Anka klockan tre på julafton.
english: Donald Duck at three on Christmas Eve.
grammar: Watching Disney's Christmas Special at 3 PM is a sacred Swedish tradition.

english: We sing drinking songs at festive meals.
The frog dance at midsummer is mandatory.
grammar: Children and adults hop like frogs around the maypole — delightfully silly.

english: The northern lights are best seen in Norrland.
The midnight sun makes the nights bright.
The polar nights make the days dark.
grammar: In northern Sweden, the sun doesn't rise for weeks in winter.

target: Bastu är populärt i hela Norden.
english: Sauna is popular throughout the Nordics.
grammar: Public bathing culture in Sweden dates back to Viking times.

english: Winter bathing is a growing trend.
grammar: Swimming in icy water followed by a sauna — a Nordic wellness tradition.

target: Vi plockar bär och svamp på hösten.
english: We pick berries and mushrooms in autumn.

target: Knäckebröd finns i alla svenska hushåll.
english: Crispbread is in every Swedish household.

target: Dalahästen är en symbol för Sverige.
english: The Dala horse is a symbol of Sweden.
grammar: Hand-painted wooden horses from Dalarna — iconic Swedish folk art.

target: IKEA har spridit svenska koncept globalt.
english: IKEA has spread Swedish concepts globally.

target: Sverige är känt för sin design.
english: Sweden is known for its design.

target: Astrid Lindgren är Sveriges mest älskade författare.
english: Astrid Lindgren is Sweden's most beloved author.
ABBA put Sweden on the music map.

target: Ingvar Kamprad grundade IKEA i Småland.
english: Ingvar Kamprad founded IKEA in Småland.

target: Selma Lagerlöf var den första kvinnliga Nobelpristagaren i litteratur.
english: Selma Lagerlöf was the first female Nobel laureate in literature.
Alfred Nobel established the Nobel Prize.
The mining town of Kiruna is being moved.
grammar: Kiruna is literally being relocated because mining has destabilized the ground.

english: Gotland is a popular vacation island.
Visby has a well-preserved medieval town.

target: Lund är en av Sveriges äldsta universitetsstäder.
english: Lund is one of Sweden's oldest university cities.

target: Ishotellet i Jukkasjärvi byggs varje vinter.
english: The Ice Hotel in Jukkasjärvi is built every winter.
The King's Trail stretches through Lapland.

target: Samer är urbefolkningen i norra Skandinavien.
english: The Sami are the indigenous people of northern Scandinavia.

target: Samisk kultur innefattar renskötsel och jojk.
english: Sami culture includes reindeer herding and joik.
grammar: Joik is a traditional Sami form of singing — deeply spiritual and cultural.

target: Ombudsman är ett svenskt ord som spridits.
english: Ombudsman is a Swedish word that has spread.
grammar: One of few Swedish words used internationally.

target: Den svenska modellen kombinerar marknad och välfärd.
english: The Swedish model combines market and welfare.

target: Fackförbund har stort inflytande i Sverige.
english: Labor unions have great influence in Sweden.
The fika break is established in working life.

target: Semesterlagen ger rätt till fem veckors semester.
english: The vacation law gives the right to five weeks' vacation.
grammar: By law, Swedish workers get 25 vacation days per year.

english: The Convention on the Rights of the Child is Swedish law since 2020.

target: Könsneutrala pronomen som hen används allt mer.
english: Gender-neutral pronouns like hen are increasingly used.
grammar: "Hen" was added to the Swedish dictionary in 2015.

target: Pappamånaden uppmuntrar pappor att vara hemma.
english: The daddy month encourages fathers to stay home.

target: Öppen förskola är gratis för alla.
english: Open preschool is free for everyone.
Child health centers follow children's development.
grammar: BVC (Barnavårdscentralen) provides free healthcare for all children.

target: SFI erbjuder gratis svenska för invandrare.
english: SFI offers free Swedish for immigrants.
grammar: Swedish for Immigrants — a comprehensive integration program.

target: Vattenfall och andra myndigheter är statligt ägda.
english: Vattenfall and other agencies are state-owned.
An early café visit to the Christmas market.

target: Luciatåget i kyrkan rör många till tårar.
english: The Lucia procession in church moves many to tears.

target: Advent firas med ljusstakar i fönstren.
english: Advent is celebrated with candle holders in the windows.
grammar: Triangle-shaped electric candelabras (adventsljusstake) in every window — a winter tradition.

english: New Year's celebration includes the poem Nyårsklockan.
grammar: Tennyson's "Ring Out, Wild Bells" is read on Swedish TV every New Year's Eve.

english: May Day is celebrated with demonstrations.
